reviews.review_msg: Dr. Perl is a seasoned, well-read professional. He is kind, intellectual, and can be (perhaps surprisingly?) quite funny. He values student input during seminar discussions. The class clearly respects him.

Perl is a good prof, although he says little in this class. Most students in this class weren't articulate. This seminar is left entirely up to the students to "teach," which most can't do. Most students don't do all the homework and can't discuss the material (they try to fake it though). It was a lame seminar. If you're smart, avoid this class.

An unorthodox teaching style to say the least. She is almost completely unresponsive to criticism or questions during lecture, but is also perfectly willing to give tons of help outside of class hours. At least for this class the instruction itself was fairly useless; she gave only 4 lectures, the rest of the course being one big group assignment.
